title: Spicy Tibs with Rhubarb Chutney
description: A flavorful and spicy tib's dish made with tenderized beef, served with a sweet and tangy rhubarb chutney. This Ethiopian-inspired recipe is perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Beef (sirloin or ribeye)
ingredients.notes: Tenderize with a meat mallet or a rolling pin

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook until they start to caramelize, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced beef and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the berbere spice and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Transfer the beef mixture to a plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil.
Add the diced rhubarb and cook until it starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
Add the brown sugar, apple cider vinegar, and a pinch of salt. Cook for 2 minutes, until the rhubarb is tender.
Return the beef mixture to the skillet and stir to combine with the rhubarb chutney.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the flavors have melded together.
Serve hot over injera or with some crusty bread.
